title: Brownfield gap entry
description: AF-2026-05-30-2 / Test10 F-11. Canonical gap-entry schema shipped for the 7 brownfield scanners (doc-code, hardcoded, integration-seam, runtime-behavior, security, sbom-completeness, call-graph) so Phase-7 dedup can match overlapping findings reliably. Prior to this schema each scanner invented its own field shape (e.g. SEC-001 vs GAP-ISEAM-001 for the same underlying issue); the dedup was forced to do best-effort substring matching and under-merged true overlaps. Adopters mint a stable `gap_id` per the convention below and dedup keys off (category, evidence.file, evidence.line_range).

gap_id.description: Stable identifier. Convention: `{SCANNER}-{NNN}` where SCANNER is the producing scanner short name (DC for doc-code, HC for hardcoded, ISEAM for integration-seam, RB for runtime-behavior, SEC for security, SBOM for sbom-completeness, CG for call-graph) and NNN is a 3-digit zero-padded sequence within that scanner's output. Phase-7 dedup MAY rewrite the gap_id when merging duplicates but MUST record the original ids in the merged entry's `aliases:` list.

category.description: Coarse classification used as a primary dedup key. Two entries that share `category` + `evidence.file` + overlapping `evidence.line_range` are presumed duplicates.

severity.description: ADR-037 severity vocabulary. CRITICAL findings halt downstream gates under ADR-063/067 (with the YOLO downgrade documented on the consuming phase).

title.description: One-line human-readable summary. Used in dedup display and the Phase-7 consolidated-gaps.md table.

description.description: Multi-line prose detail. Optional — many findings are self-explanatory from title + evidence.

evidence.description: The fact pattern that produced this finding. Required so the reader can verify against the codebase without re-running the scanner.

file.description: Project-relative path to the evidence file (or the artifact the finding refers to, e.g. `prd.md` for a doc-code-drift finding).

line_range.description: Line range in the file the evidence covers, e.g. `42-58` or `12` for a single line. Used by Phase-7 dedup overlap detection.

snippet.description: Optional excerpt of the offending content (max ~2KB). Helpful for fast review without round-tripping to the file.

tool.description: Tool/adapter that produced the finding (e.g. `grype`, `semgrep`, `gaia-doc-code-scanner`). Used for provenance + scan-fidelity attribution per Test10 §7 C3 banner.

suggested_action.description: Optional remediation hint. Triage and tech-debt-review may promote this into a story Origin section.

stack.description: Detected stack the finding belongs to (e.g. `python`, `node`, `java`, `bash`). Helps Phase-7 dedup avoid cross-stack false-merges and lets the consolidated-gaps banner attribute findings per-stack.

aliases.description: Phase-7 dedup output. When two scanners produced equivalent findings (e.g. SEC-001 ≡ GAP-ISEAM-001), the merged entry records both original ids here so a triage reviewer can trace back to either scanner.

confidence.description: Optional confidence assessment from the producing scanner. Helps dedup tie-break when two findings share the same evidence overlap.

metadata.description: Scanner-specific metadata pass-through. Phase-7 dedup MUST NOT key off this field — it is informational only.
